title: teline bracteolata
intro: Teline bracteolata, also known as the broom or broom-like plant, is a small, annual legume native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its delicate yellow flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of coumarins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Teline bracteolata include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Teline bracteolata can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and overall well-being.
